mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.25 percentage points over 30 days
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2019-01-15 indicates aggressive easing cycle
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following tightening reversal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk, causing equity investors to demand a higher risk premium and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.4% as of 2019-01-15 indicates elevated unemployment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales measured by RSAFS indicate weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress in response to lower growth forecasts.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 1.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 424481 million dollars as of 2019-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward returns < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production measured by INDPRO exhibits a sustained 3-month declining trend, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced corporate capacity utilization that depresses earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for three consecutive months with cumulative decline > 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.9462 as of 2019-01-15 shows weakness relative to 2017 baseline
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO 3-month change and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST and retail sales measured by RSAFS jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth exceeding 2%, equity investors increase risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase by more than 2% month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 836 thousand units and RSAFS at 424481 million dollars as of 2019-01-15
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of co-triggered instances, or joint signal has lower predictive power than either series alone (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment measured by PAYEMS and unemployment rate measured by UNRATE move in opposite directions when labor market tightens (PAYEMS growth > 150k/month and UNRATE declining). This joint signal indicates strong earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150 thousand in a month while UNRATE declines by at least 0.1 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136038 thousand and UNRATE at 7.4% as of 2019-01-15
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong job creation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 56% of co-triggered instances, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) when controlling for individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.5%, creating a negative real interest rate regime that incentivizes equity investment over cash, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as portfolio rebalancing occurs.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS is below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 233.318 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2019-01-15
regime_hint: negative real rates regime with monetary accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (real rate proxy) has lower predictive power than FEDFUNDS alone (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP measured by GDPC1 exhibits quarter-over-quarter growth below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle deceleration that reduces forward earnings growth expectations. This regime-conditional mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days only when growth is decelerating from prior quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 1.5% or declines by more than 0.5 percentage points from prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15648.7 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2018-10-15 indicates late-cycle positioning
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 48% of triggered instances during late-cycle regimes, or Rank IC < 0.04 when GDPC1 growth is decelerating.
